name: canonify-viewspecs
description: Author Canonify ViewSpecs — the declarative UI primitive. Compose catalog pieces (ObjectTypes + ActionTypes) into transport-agnostic views built from 10 block types (table, detail, panel, button, composite, metric, chart, file, board, queue), then declare and apply them to an org via view_specs.declare. Read SKILL.md first.

resources.title: The 10 block types
resources.description: Field-by-field reference for table, detail, panel, button, composite, metric, chart, file, board, and queue — the closed discriminated union the renderer dispatches on. Each block type with a full JSON example and an ASCII layout diagram.

resources.title: Binding and sources
resources.description: How a block binds to the catalog: ObjectTypeSource (objectType + filter + sort), AggregateSource (aggregate + groupBy), column-ref grammar (shorthand dot-traversal + link aggregates), filter scalars and range predicates, and the $context.* render-context grammar.

resources.title: Master-detail recipe
resources.description: Compose a list that drills into a record detail: a table master plus a detail block that reads its record id from $context, with field-by-field property mapping and label overrides. Full JSON + ASCII layout.

resources.title: Metrics and grouped tables
resources.description: Aggregate-backed views: scalar metric (KPI) tiles and grouped summary tables. count / sum / avg / min / max with groupBy, the property-required rules, and a KPI-dashboard composite. Full JSON + ASCII layout.

resources.title: Forms and actions
resources.description: Wire ActionTypes into a view: panel (action form), button (one-tap invoke), presetParams + $context refs, submit labels, and showWhen UX conditioning. Includes the view_specs.declare authoring action. Full JSON + ASCII layout.

resources.title: Composition and apply
resources.description: Nest blocks with composite, build modal-dialog and embedded-table patterns, then declare + apply the finished ViewSpec to an org isomorphically over REST / CLI / MCP via view_specs.declare. Full JSON + ASCII layout + the cross-transport apply path.
